articles.title: Lopez becomes NBA's 1st Mexico-born 1st-rounder
articles.summary: Karim Lopez became the first player born in Mexico to be selected in the first round of the NBA draft, being selected with the No. 21 pick to the Detroit Pistons before being traded to the Memphis Grizzlies on Tuesday night.

articles.title: Johnson reunites with May, goes No. 9 to Mavs
articles.summary: The Mavericks picked Michigan forward Morez Johnson Jr. with the No. 9 pick, reuniting him with Dusty May less than six hours after he was announced as their new head coach.

articles.title: Jayhawks' Peterson lands with Jazz at No. 2 pick
articles.summary: Kansas guard Darryn Peterson, who had been considered a potential No. 1 pick, wound up with the Jazz as the second pick of the 2026 NBA draft.

articles.title: Hammon won't apologize, says Brunson 'outlier'
articles.summary: Aces coach Becky Hammon had a change of heart regarding her prior criticism of Knicks star Jalen Brunson, saying Tuesday night that her "opinion was wrong." But she's not apologizing for having it.

articles.title: Sources: Randle off to Nets as part of 3-team deal...

articles.title: Golden Knights owner launching NBA Vegas bid
articles.summary: Golden Knights founder and majority owner Bill Foley announced Monday that he is launching a bid to bring an NBA franchise to Las Vegas.

articles.title: Sources: Heat land Giannis in blockbuster trade
articles.summary: The Bucks are trading Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis to the Heat for Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kasparas Jakucionis, three first-round picks (including No. 13 in Tuesday's draft), one pick swap and one second-rounder, per sources.

articles.title: How the Knicks and hip-hop united New York
articles.summary: The last time the Knicks won a title, hip-hop was born. Fifty-three years later, New York's rappers took a victory lap.

articles.title: Sources: U-M's May finalizing deal to coach Mavs
articles.summary: Michigan coach Dusty May is finalizing a deal to take the Dallas Mavericks' head coaching job, sources told ESPN, an axis-shifting move that significantly alters the college and NBA landscapes.
